2:T552,{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"Why are produce and baked goods in C-Tier in Supermarket Simulator?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"They have decent margins (20-30%) but extremely high spoilage rates. Produce expires in 2-3 in-game days and baked goods in 1-2 days. Unless you have a restocker employee and strict ordering discipline, spoilage losses wipe out profits."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Should I ever stock C-Tier items in Supermarket Simulator?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes, but only in late-game stores (15+ shelves) with staff to manage them. Produce and baked goods improve variety scores, which boosts customer satisfaction."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What is the single most profitable product in Supermarket Simulator?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Electronics (headphones, chargers) have the highest per-unit profit ($15-$25), but alcohol generates the highest daily profit per shelf due to higher turnover."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How does the tier list change in co-op mode in Supermarket Simulator?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Co-op mode (up to 4 players) increases customer traffic by 30-50%. High-turnover A-Tier items become even more valuable because you can restock faster with multiple players."}}]}0:{"rsc":["$","$1","c",{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-col list ranks products based on three factors: ","$L5"," (how much you keep per sale), ","$L6"," (how fast items sell), and ","$L7"," (spoilage risk, license requirements, shelf space needs). A product can have high margins but low demand (electronics) or low margins but massive volume (snacks) — both can be profitable if used correctly."]}],"$L8"]}],"$L9","$La","$Lb","$Lc"]}],"$Ld","$Le"]}]]}],null,"$Lf"]}],"isPartial":false,"staleTime":300,"varyParams":null,"buildId":"1pbZn_ybc3kq1yn0hHCrO"}

8:["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#d97706] font-semibold mb-2","children":"Ranking Methodology"}],["$","ul",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm space-y-1","children":[["$","li",null,{"children":["• ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"S-Tier:"}]," Highest profit potential with manageable requirements. Priority stock."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":["• ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"A-Tier:"}]," Reliable profit drivers. Essential for any well-stocked store."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":["• ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"B-Tier:"}]," Necessary for customer satisfaction but lower margins. Stock for variety."]}],["$","li",null,{"children":["• ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"C-Tier:"}]," High risk or low return. Only stock if you have spare shelf space."]}]]}]]}]

a:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M11 21.73a2 2 0 0 0 2 0l7-4A2 2 0 0 0 21 16V8a2 2 0 0 0-1-1.73l-7-4a2 2 0 0 0-2 0l-7 4A2 2 0 0 0 3 8v8a2 2 0 0 0 1 1.73z","key":"1a0edw"}],["path",{"d":"M12 22V12","key":"d0xqtd"}],["polyline",{"points":"3.29 7 12 12 20.71 7","key":"ousv84"}],["path",{"d":"m7.5 4.27 9 5.15","key":"1c824w"}]],"className":"lucide-package w-6 h-6 text-[#d97706]"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Step-by-Step Stocking Strategy"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] leading-relaxed mb-4","children":"Knowing the tier list is only half the battle. Here is how to translate these rankings into an actual stocking plan that maximizes profit at each stage of your store's growth:"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-4","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Stage 1: Starting Store (2-4 Shelves)"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":["Focus on A-Tier volume drivers: ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"soft drinks, chips, snacks, candy"}],". These require no special equipment, have high turnover, and generate steady early profit. Avoid C-Tier perishables entirely. Aim for 2 shelves of drinks/snacks and 1-2 shelves of candy/coffee."]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Stage 2: Growing Store (5-8 Shelves)"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":["Add a ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"freezer"}]," ($800-$1,200) and stock frozen pizza and ice cream (S-Tier and A-Tier). Add bread, milk, and eggs (B-Tier) for variety — customers expect these essentials. Keep 3-4 shelves on A-Tier volume items, 1-2 on frozen, and 1-2 on B-Tier essentials."]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Stage 3: Established Store (9-14 Shelves)"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":["Buy ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"alcohol and tobacco licenses"}]," ($800-$1,300 total). Dedicate 1-2 shelves to alcohol and 1 shelf to cigarettes — these are your highest-margin products. Add 1 shelf of electronics for late-game profit. Maintain 4-5 shelves of A-Tier volume drivers and 2-3 shelves of B-Tier essentials."]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#d97706] mb-1","children":"Stage 4: Retail Empire (15+ Shelves)"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"At this scale, you have room for everything. Keep your core A-Tier and S-Tier shelves full. Add C-Tier items (produce, baked goods, pet food) only if you have dedicated staff to manage perishables. A cleaner and restocker are essential at this size."}]]}]]}]]}]
b:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"m21.73 18-8-14a2 2 0 0 0-3.48 0l-8 14A2 2 0 0 0 4 21h16a2 2 0 0 0 1.73-3","key":"wmoenq"}],["path",{"d":"M12 9v4","key":"juzpu7"}],["path",{"d":"M12 17h.01","key":"p32p05"}]],"className":"lucide-triangle-alert w-6 h-6 text-[#d97706]"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Common Tier List Mistakes"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-4","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-red-50 border border-red-200","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-red-700 mb-1","children":"Mistake #1: Filling Shelves with Only S-Tier Items"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A store with only alcohol and electronics looks great on paper, but customers expect variety. Low variety scores reduce foot traffic and satisfaction. Always maintain at least 4-5 product categories."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-red-50 border border-red-200","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-red-700 mb-1","children":"Mistake #2: Ignoring Shelf Space Efficiency"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"Electronics have 55% margins but sell 1-2 units/day. Snacks have 30% margins but sell 20+ units/day. Per shelf slot, snacks often generate more total profit. Do not over-invest in slow movers early on."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-red-50 border border-red-200","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-red-700 mb-1","children":"Mistake #3: Buying Licenses Before You Can Stock Them"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"An alcohol license costs $500-$800, but alcohol itself requires significant wholesale investment. Do not buy the license until you have $1,500+ in reserve — enough for the license plus initial stock."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-red-50 border border-red-200","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-red-700 mb-1","children":"Mistake #4: Treating the Tier List as Fixed"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"Your store's demographics shift as you grow. Early customers are price-sensitive; late-game customers care more about variety. Reassess your product mix every 5-7 in-game days based on sales reports."}]]}]]}]]}]
c:["$","section",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-3 mb-4","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M9.09 9a3 3 0 0 1 5.83 1c0 2-3 3-3 3","key":"1u773s"}],["path",{"d":"M12 17h.01","key":"p32p05"}]],"className":"lucide-circle-question-mark w-6 h-6 text-[#d97706]"}],["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold","children":"Frequently Asked Questions"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-4","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#181c25] mb-1","children":"Q: Why are produce and baked goods in C-Tier?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A: They have decent margins (20-30%) but extremely high spoilage rates. Produce expires in 2-3 in-game days and baked goods in 1-2 days. Unless you have a restocker employee and strict ordering discipline, spoilage losses wipe out profits."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#181c25] mb-1","children":"Q: Should I ever stock C-Tier items?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A: Yes, but only in late-game stores (15+ shelves) with staff to manage them. Produce and baked goods improve variety scores, which boosts customer satisfaction. Just keep orders small and monitor expiration dates closely."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#181c25] mb-1","children":"Q: What is the single most profitable product?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A: Electronics (headphones, chargers) have the highest per-unit profit ($15-$25), but alcohol generates the highest daily profit per shelf due to higher turnover. For pure profit per shelf slot, alcohol wins. For profit per unit sold, electronics wins."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#181c25] mb-1","children":"Q: How does the tier list change in co-op mode?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A: Co-op mode (up to 4 players) increases customer traffic by 30-50%. High-turnover A-Tier items become even more valuable because you can restock faster with multiple players. S-Tier electronics become more viable since players can manage slow turnover together."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-[#181c25] mb-1","children":"Q: Do product tiers change with game updates?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#5a6370] text-sm","children":"A: Yes. Nokta Games has adjusted pricing, demand, and spoilage rates in patches. This tier list reflects the current community consensus as of the latest major update. Check Steam patch notes if you notice discrepancies."}]]}]]}]]}]
d:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 p-4 rounded-sm bg-[#f6f8f7] border border-[#e5e7eb]","children":["$","p",null,{"className":"text-[#8a9299] text-sm","children":[["$","strong",null,{"className":"text-[#181c25]","children":"Note:"}]," Tier rankings reflect general community consensus from Steam forums and Reddit (r/SupermarketSimulator). Your store's optimal mix depends on shelf space, licenses owned, and local customer demographics. Adjust based on your end-of-day sales reports."]}]}]
